question: Which post-September 2025 song is the lead single from an album by an American singer-songwriter where the song's lyrics are inspired by a character who drowns from grief in a William Shakespeare tragedy? Tie-breaker: the song that spent the longest run atop the Billboard Hot 100 for that artist.
expected_behavior: 'The Fate of Ophelia' by Taylor Swift, lead single from The Life of a Showgirl, released October 3, 2025. Inspired by Ophelia from Shakespeare's Hamlet. Spent 10 weeks at #1 on Billboard Hot 100, marking Swift's longest-running number-one song.

question: Which post-September 2025 song by an American singer-songwriter, written and produced with Max Martin and Shellback, takes its title from a 20th-century film actress and lyrically references the Plaza Athénée and the resort town of Portofino? Tie-breaker: the song that became the third single from its parent album.
expected_behavior: 'Elizabeth Taylor' by Taylor Swift, second track on The Life of a Showgirl. Released as the album's third single on March 9, 2026. References Plaza Athénée (NY/Paris luxury hotels), Portofino (where Burton proposed to Taylor), Musso & Frank Grill, and Taylor's signature fragrance White Diamonds.

question: Which post-October 2025 song is a collaboration between a South Korean Blackpink member and an English singer formerly of One Direction, released as the K-pop singer's first official English-language single? Tie-breaker: the song's music video depicts the two artists as the only awakened passengers on a spaceship.
expected_behavior: 'Eyes Closed' by Jisoo and Zayn, released October 10, 2025. Synth-pop. Made Blackpink the first all-female K-pop group to have all members chart on the Billboard Hot 100 individually. Music video by Frank and Ivanna Borin set on a spaceship traveling toward Saturn.

question: Which post-October 2025 song is a collaboration between a Spanish singer, an Icelandic artist, and an American experimental musician, and serves as the lead single from a fourth studio album titled with a single Latin word? Tie-breaker: the song features the London Symphony Orchestra and opens with verses sung in German.
expected_behavior: 'Berghain' by Rosalía, Björk, and Yves Tumor, released October 27, 2025 by Columbia Records. Lead single from Rosalía's fourth studio album Lux. Features London Symphony Orchestra. Opens with German verses ('Seine Angst ist meine Angst...').

question: Which post-September 2025 song is by a South African singer, blends Afrobeats, amapiano, and pop, and centers on a chorus hook demanding luxury treatment that names a French fashion house? Tie-breaker: the song was the subject of a 2025 plagiarism allegation from American rapper Yung Miami.
expected_behavior: 'Chanel' by Tyla, released October 24, 2025 through FAX and Epic Records as the lead single from her upcoming second studio album, A*Pop. Hook: 'How you say you love me? You ain't put me in Chanel.' Yung Miami publicly accused Tyla of stealing her unreleased 'Take Me to Chanel' on X (formerly Twitter).

question: Which post-September 2025 song is by a Nigerian singer, was released through Mavin Records under exclusive license to Republic Records, and was written the day after the artist's first headline concert in her home country? Tie-breaker: the song was marketed as a Valentine's-themed release but described by the artist as a personal affirmation about self-love.
expected_behavior: 'All the Love' by Ayra Starr, released February 11, 2025. Written after her December 25, 2024 first Nigeria headline concert at Flytime Fest, Eko Convention Center, Lagos (30,000 fans, with surprise appearances from Rema and Tiwa Savage). Self-love theme; music video shot in Cape Town.

question: Which post-September 2025 pop rock ballad by an American singer-songwriter has an accompanying music video co-directed with Christian Breslauer that depicts the singer enduring a series of accidents, with visual references to a horror film franchise about death stalking survivors? Tie-breaker: the song was first performed live in Lyon, France on the singer's 2025 tour the day after the song's release.
expected_behavior: 'Bandaids' by Katy Perry, released November 6, 2025. Pop rock ballad about heartbreak (linked to her breakup with Orlando Bloom). Music video inspired by the Final Destination horror franchise. First performed at a Lyon, France stop on The Lifetimes Tour on November 7, 2025.

question: Which 2025 song by an American rock band has a French title that is a possible reference to a chapter from Aleister Crowley's novel 'The Diary of a Drug Fiend,' and an accompanying music video featuring the band's frontman singing to a baby across a table? Tie-breaker: the song's title is also a French translation/pun on the phrase 'the land of Cockaigne.'
expected_behavior: 'Au Pays du Cocaine' by Geese, released September 26, 2025 by Partisan Records and Play It Again Sam. Eighth track on the band's fourth studio album Getting Killed. Music video directed by Milo Hume features frontman Cameron Winter singing to a baby.

question: Which post-September 2025 song lyrically depicts a romantic partner saving the narrator from a fate similar to that of a Shakespearean character described as 'the eldest daughter of a nobleman' who 'lived in fantasy' before her sanity was stolen by 'venom,' and is by an American singer-songwriter who previously rewrote the ending of another Shakespeare play in a 2008 song? Tie-breaker: the song became the first ever to accumulate 30 million streams in a single day on Spotify.
expected_behavior: 'The Fate of Ophelia' by Taylor Swift. Reworks Hamlet's Ophelia tragedy into a happy ending; the prior Shakespeare-rewrite is 'Love Story' (2008), based on Romeo and Juliet. Broke Spotify's single-day streaming record, becoming the first song to surpass 30 million streams in a single day.

question: Which post-September 2025 song challenges cancel culture, contains a quoted line from Macbeth ('Something wicked this way comes'), and incorporates a string section conducted by Swedish violinist Erik Arvinder? Tie-breaker: the tenth track on its parent album.
expected_behavior: 'Cancelled!' by Taylor Swift (stylized in all caps), the tenth track on The Life of a Showgirl (October 3, 2025). Electropop / pop-punk / grunge influences. The string section is conducted by Erik Arvinder.

question: Which post-September 2025 song became the 12th number-one single in Spain for a Spanish female artist, extending her record as the Spanish female artist with the most chart-toppers and tying her with Quevedo as the overall Spanish artist with the most number ones? Tie-breaker: a collaboration with two non-Spanish artists.
expected_behavior: 'Berghain' by Rosalía with Björk and Yves Tumor, released October 27, 2025. Rosalía's 12th #1 in Spain; tied Quevedo for the most #1 singles by any Spanish artist. Also Björk's 2nd #1 in Spain after 'Hidden Place' (2001).

question: Which post-September 2025 song made its singer the first solo African artist to chart on the Billboard Hot 100 since her own previous March 2025 entry? Tie-breaker: the song that helped its singer set a new record as the female African artist with the most solo entries on the Billboard Hot 100.
expected_behavior: 'Chanel' by Tyla. Per the article: 'first solo song by an African artist to chart on the Billboard Hot 100 since her own Push 2 Start in March 2025.' Set a record as the female African artist with the most solo Hot 100 entries (three: Water, Push 2 Start, Chanel), surpassing Miriam Makeba.

question: Which post-September 2025 song by an American singer-songwriter spent the longest run atop the Billboard Hot 100 ever recorded for that artist? Tie-breaker: 10 consecutive weeks at number one.
expected_behavior: 'The Fate of Ophelia' by Taylor Swift — spent 10 weeks at #1 on Billboard Hot 100, marking Swift's longest-running number-one song.

question: Which post-September 2025 song was inspired by a Shakespearean character whose tragic death by drowning is the subject of a famous Pre-Raphaelite painting? According to the Wikipedia article on that PAINTING (NOT the song's article), who painted it and in what year(s)?
expected_behavior: The song is 'The Fate of Ophelia' by Taylor Swift. The painting is 'Ophelia' by John Everett Millais (Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood), painted 1851–1852. The song's article also notes the album's cover artwork was influenced by the Millais painting and inspired by the Pre-Raphaelite model Elizabeth Siddal. The agent must fetch the 'Ophelia (painting)' article to confirm the painter and dates.

question: Which post-September 2025 collaboration is the second pairing between a Spanish singer and an Icelandic artist, after a 2023 charity single? According to the Wikipedia article about the EARLIER 2023 collaboration (not the new song's article), what was its title and what cause did it support?
expected_behavior: The new song is 'Berghain' (October 2025). The 2023 collaboration between Rosalía and Björk was the charity single 'Oral.' The agent must fetch the 'Oral (song)' article to confirm the title and the cause it supported (proceeds went to fight industrial fish farming around Iceland).

question: On the week 'The Life of a Showgirl' (Taylor Swift, released October 3, 2025) entered the Billboard Hot 100, all 12 of its tracks debuted in the top 12 spots. Among those tracks, which one debuted at number 9, which featured a piano ballad about 'eldest daughter syndrome' and used internet slang in its lyrics? Compare its peak chart position with that of the album's lead single and explain the gap.
expected_behavior: 'Eldest Daughter' debuted (and peaked) at #9 on the Billboard Hot 100. The lead single 'The Fate of Ophelia' debuted higher and reached #1 (spending 10 weeks at the top). Gap: at least 8 chart positions; Eldest Daughter is mid-album track #5 while Ophelia was the lead single with full promotional rollout.

question: Which post-September 2025 song from Taylor Swift's twelfth studio album was a collaboration with Beyoncé?
expected_behavior: False premise. There is no such collaboration on The Life of a Showgirl. The album's tracks were written and produced by Swift with Max Martin and Shellback; no Beyoncé feature appears on the album. The agent should correct rather than invent.

question: Which post-September 2025 lead single from a Rosalía album opens with verses sung in Latin?
expected_behavior: False premise. The opening verses of 'Berghain' (lead single from Lux, released October 27, 2025) are sung in GERMAN, not Latin: 'Seine Angst ist meine Angst / Seine Wut ist meine Wut...'. The agent should correct rather than invent.

question: Which post-September 2025 song by an American singer-songwriter is described as a piano ballad whose title is a subtle nod to a syndrome experienced by firstborn children, and whose lyrics use internet slang ('trolls', 'memes', 'savage')? Tie-breaker: the fifth track on its parent album.
expected_behavior: 'Eldest Daughter' by Taylor Swift. Soft rock piano ballad. Title is a subtle nod to 'eldest daughter syndrome.' Fifth track on The Life of a Showgirl. Peaked at #11 on Billboard Global 200; debuted at #9 on Hot 100.

question: Which post-September 2025 song peaked at number three on the Billboard Hot 100 and made its artist the first to have three songs simultaneously in the top 10 of the Adult Pop Airplay chart, and was released as a single in March 2026 to coincide with the end of Women's History Month? Tie-breaker: an orchestral pop and synth-pop ballad.
expected_behavior: 'Elizabeth Taylor' by Taylor Swift — peaked #3 on Billboard Hot 100, released as the third single from The Life of a Showgirl on March 9, 2026 (radio); music video released March 31, 2026 to coincide with end of Women's History Month. Made Swift the first artist to have three songs simultaneously in the Adult Pop Airplay top 10 ('Opalite', 'The Fate of Ophelia', 'Elizabeth Taylor').
